site_purpose: Free business resources, guides, and tools for nutrition practitioners. Anchors the 'business side of practice' content for the LAUNCH and GROW programs. Not a sales page — a content hub that stands alone and also funnels to structured programs when practitioners are ready.
hero_subhead: Free resources, guides, and tools to help nutrition practitioners turn their education into a sustainable practice.

brand_voice.tone: Direct, operator-to-operator. Sean speaks as someone who's built and run real businesses — gyms, swim schools, a BJJ academy, a clinical nutrition practice — not as a business guru or course seller. The tone is military discipline meets wellness entrepreneurship: structured, specific, no-fluff, and grounded in lived experience. Think mentor-as-operator, not coach-as-celebrity.
brand_voice.site_tone_note: This site is a resource hub, not a coaching pitch. Copy should be helpful and generous — giving away real value, not teasing content behind a paywall. Programs are mentioned as natural next steps, not hard sells.
personality_traits: Results-oriented — every resource leads to a concrete next step, not motivational platitudes
Systems-thinking — builds frameworks and repeatable processes, not one-off advice
Anti-fluff — cuts through vague coaching language with specific, actionable guidance
Operator mindset — treats building a practice like running a business, because it is one
Specific over vague — names the exact problem, the exact system, the exact outcome
Generous with knowledge — freely shares tools, frameworks, and resources that practitioners can use immediately
Mentor-as-peer — walks alongside practitioners, not above them; earned authority through doing
Unconventional path — Naval Academy, submarines, multiple business ventures before clinical nutrition; proves there's no single 'right path'
language_patterns: Frames business building as a learnable skill, not a personality trait ('business sense isn't innate — it's a system you install')
Confronts the credentials-vs-career gap directly ('Your MS didn't teach you how to find your first paying client')
Names specific business systems rather than abstract concepts ('client intake workflow,' 'pricing architecture,' 'revenue model')
Uses military/operational metaphors sparingly ('mission planning,' 'systems check') to frame business strategy
Bridges clinical credibility with business savvy ('You already know how to help people. Now learn how to build the practice that lets you.')
Presents programs as a natural next step, not a sales pitch ('These resources stand alone. But if you want structured mentorship, explore our programs.')
Draws from personal experience: built a gym in DC, launched a swim school, founded a BJJ academy — each one a lesson in entrepreneurship

key_frameworks: The Credential Gap: clinical programs train excellent practitioners but don't teach business — no pricing, no marketing, no client acquisition, no systems. This site bridges that gap with free resources.
From Credential to Career: the tagline and core thesis — turning education into a sustainable practice is a distinct skill set that can be learned.
Operator Mindset: treat your practice like a business from day one. Revenue systems, client pipelines, professional infrastructure — not 'follow your passion and the money will come.'
Systems Before Scale: build repeatable processes first (intake, pricing, scheduling, follow-up), then grow. Most practitioners try to scale chaos.
The Full Practitioner: clinical skill is only half the equation. Business acumen, professional confidence, and sustainable operations complete the picture.

target_audience.primary: Nutrition practitioners (CNS, RDN, NTP) looking for free, practical business resources to build or grow their private practice
target_audience.secondary: Practitioners in LAUNCH or GROW programs who use this hub as a companion resource library
target_audience.tertiary: Health and wellness professionals outside nutrition who recognize the credential-to-career gap in their own field
target_audience.psychographic: Clinically confident but business-uncertain. They know their craft but feel lost when it comes to pricing, marketing, finding clients, and building a sustainable revenue model. Looking for practical tools they can use now — not another sales funnel.

ecosystem_role.relationship_to_hq: This site is the business-content arm of holisticconsultinghq.com. Resources here support the LAUNCH and GROW program curriculum. 1:1 coaching lives under MASTER on HQ, not here.
ecosystem_role.program_mentions: Programs (LAUNCH, GROW) are referenced as natural next steps in a Programs CTA section — not hard sells. Links go to holisticconsultinghq.com.

key_differentiators: Sean's background: Naval Academy graduate, submarine officer (8 years), gym owner in DC, swim school founder, Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu academy founder, clinical nutritionist, MS in Clinical Nutrition, MS in Herbal Product Manufacturing, adjunct faculty at Maryland University of Integrative Health
Not a course seller — Sean has built and operated 5+ real businesses across different industries before entering wellness entrepreneurship
Free-first approach — this site gives away real tools and resources, not gated content teasers
Connected to HQ's full ecosystem — practitioners can access career programs (LAUNCH/GROW/MASTER), herbalism education, and a professional community through Holistic Consulting
University-level teaching credibility — Sean teaches business development at MUIH, grounding his guidance in academic rigor, not just personal anecdote
